ideas.detail: expand-via-oa resolves each referenced work with one HTTP round trip (openalex.py fetch_references) and even fetches each seed twice; canon-atlas re-queries all 25 subfields on every run. OpenAlex supports filter=openalex_id:W1|W2|... batching (up to 50 ids per call) and its records change slowly, so a sqlite- or json-backed cache keyed by work id with a TTL would cut request volume ~50x, make --all-seeds-since runs feasible on the anonymous pool, and make CI runs reproducible when OpenAlex flakes.

ideas.title: Status-aware fetch API shared by both source modules
ideas.detail: Three separate defects (OA enrich mislabeling, S2 expand silent-429, genealogy silent-400) all stem from best-effort helpers collapsing 404/429/5xx/network into None or []. Both modules already have _http_get_json_with_status; the missing piece is a public fetch_paper_with_status / fetch_references_with_status (or a tiny Result dataclass with .status) so batch CLIs stamp fetched_at only on genuine 404 and print per-row status uniformly. Cheapest path: mirror what cmd_enrich_via_s2 already does, everywhere.

ideas.title: Recorded-fixture contract tests plus a weekly live smoke job
ideas.detail: All mocked tests encode invented API shapes (ids.arxiv) and never exercise filter syntax, so the live `<=`/`>=` 400s and the dead ids.arxiv branch shipped green. Capture a handful of real OpenAlex/S2 payloads (old-style twin record, no-abstract work, merged-id redirect) as JSON fixtures, assert the exact query strings built for year_max/min_cited_by, and add a tiny scheduled CI job with OPENALEX_API_KEY that runs one keyed /works filter query with each operator and one fetch_paper — API drift then surfaces as a red cron run instead of silently wrong atlases.

ideas.title: Derive arxiv ids from the arXiv DOI (10.48550/arXiv.<id>)
ideas.detail: Every OpenAlex record of an arxiv paper carries the arxiv id verbatim in doi/ids.doi, which is more reliable than landing_page_url scraping and works for old-style, subject-class, and preprint-twin records alike. One regex on work['doi'] as the primary extraction (locations as fallback) fixes expand-via-oa, fix-arxiv-ids and canon routing in one place — and matches what the module docstring already claims to do.

ideas.title: Preprint/journal twin-record dedup for canon and coverage
ideas.detail: OpenAlex holds separate works for the arXiv-DOI record and the journal record of the same paper (verified: W6910763658 vs W2091653681 for Challet-Zhang 1997), each with its own cited_by_count and W-id. Canon lists, in_db matching (which compares single oa_paper_id values) and missing_canon routing all treat them as distinct papers, risking double-counted canon and duplicate oa:W... ingests. Cheapest safeguard: normalize by title+year (or resolve the arXiv DOI to its twin via the arxiv id) before ranking/matching, and store both W-ids on the DB row.

ideas.title: On-disk cache of OpenAlex work JSON for reproducible sweeps
ideas.detail: canon-atlas/expand/genealogy re-fetch the same works on every run (expand-via-oa even fetches each seed twice per run), so results change with API weather and rate limits, and nothing records what the API returned when an atlas PR was generated. A small sqlite/JSON cache keyed by W-id/DOI with a fetched_at timestamp would cut traffic ~50%+, make atlas artifacts reproducible/auditable, and give the rate-limit lock far fewer chances to trip mid-sweep.

ideas.title: Write-time fact-enum validation + off-enum audit command
ideas.detail: Nothing enforces the EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT's 'choose ONLY from this fixed list' contract, and coverage silently discards off-enum labels, so extraction drift is invisible. Add a validate step in extract_paper_structured that normalizes via taxonomy.canonical_fact(), maps a small synonym alias table (mirroring TAG_ALIASES), routes unknowns to 'other' while preserving the raw label in a side column, and add an arxiv_cli 'facts-audit' subcommand that lists all distinct stored fact labels with counts vs CANONICAL_FACTS (the existing stylized-fact-other command only audits 'other', not off-enum labels).

ideas.title: Persist below-threshold extraction verdicts
ideas.detail: ingest()'s min_relevance_to_keep drop path stores nothing, so scheduled CI runs re-pay LLM extraction for the same rejected papers indefinitely and extract-untagged silently resurrects them. Store the relevance_score + extracted_by_model (filter at read time via load_literature's existing min_relevance), or add a 'skipped_low_relevance' stamp checked by both ingest and extract-untagged. This is both a cost control and a gate-integrity fix.

ideas.title: Typed provider-error classification and server-hinted backoff
ideas.detail: Both llm_client and arxiv_ingest classify errors by substring-matching str(exc) — already bitten once by Groq's billing-URL upsell, still fragile (RPD vs TPD vs TPM, message rewording). The Groq/OpenAI SDKs expose exc.status_code and body['error']['code']/['type']; classify on those, and parse Groq's 'Please try again in Xs' hint (or the Retry-After header) instead of a fixed time.sleep(65). Centralize this in llm_client so arxiv_ingest's parallel classifier (a second, divergent mirror) can be deleted.

ideas.title: Circuit breaker for rate-limit storms in batch loops
ideas.detail: Neither ingest() nor cmd_extract_untagged aborts on systemic failure: after TPD/RPD exhaustion, every remaining paper still fires a doomed API call (plus up to 130s of penalty sleeps under the RPD misclassification). Track consecutive rate-limit failures and bail out of the loop with a clear summary (papers processed / remaining) after N in a row, returning a distinct exit code so CI can distinguish 'quota exhausted, resume tomorrow' from 'code broken'.

ideas.title: Recorded-response contract test suite for the extraction pipeline
ideas.detail: The tests exercise extract_paper_structured mostly via clean dry_run_response fixtures; nothing pins behavior against realistic provider pathologies. Build a fixture set of ~20 recorded/adversarial LLM responses — string-formatted lists, single-element JSON arrays, NaN/string relevance scores, off-enum facts, Japanese tokens in tags, comma-embedded tags, truncated JSON — and assert the full DB round-trip invariant (store via update_literature_extraction, reload via load_literature, compare). This would have caught the NaN→1.0 clamp, the comma round-trip split, and the enum passthrough before they reached the corpus.

ideas.title: Transaction composition API for multi-write flows
ideas.detail: Every db.py function opens its own connection and commits, so no caller (execute_proposal, ingest, idea scaffolding) can make a multi-table change atomic — the orphan-run bug is one instance of a structural gap. Cheapest path: let each function accept an optional `con: sqlite3.Connection` (commit only when it owns the connection) plus a `@contextmanager def atlas_transaction(db_path)`; then make execute_proposal's insert_run + update_proposal_status one transaction. Also enables setting WAL + busy_timeout in one place for the concurrent cron/local case.

ideas.title: Status state-machine enforcement with CHECK constraints and transition table
ideas.detail: proposals.status and ideas.status enums live only in comments; nothing prevents typos, illegal transitions (executed -> proposed), or execution-failure being conflated with human 'rejected' (propose_cli.py:281). Add CHECK(status IN (...)) via table rebuild or Python-side whitelist, a distinct 'exec_failed' status, and a small transition map asserted in update_proposal_status/update_idea. Then a one-off audit query for rows already in impossible states.

ideas.title: Paper identity layer: DOI + normalized-title dedup
ideas.detail: literature_methods has no DOI column and identity is the raw arxiv_id string, so arxiv/oa:W duplicates and manual versioned ids can double-count papers in coverage, gap mining and LLM corpus context. Cheapest path: add doi + title_norm columns (both already available from OpenAlex/arxiv payloads), backfill, add a `dedup-report` CLI that lists rows agreeing on doi or title_norm but differing in arxiv_id, and check both keys in canon_ingest before minting an oa: row.

ideas.title: Snapshot round-trip integrity test for the CI 'source of truth'
ideas.detail: data/literature_methods.json is declared the source of truth, but dump->restore->dump is lossy (12+ columns dropped, source_kind reset). Add a pytest that builds a DB with every column populated (including an oa:W row), dumps via load_literature, restores via the same code path the workflow uses (extract it into db.py as restore_literature_rows), and asserts field-for-field equality. This turns silent weekly data loss into a red test.

ideas.title: Duplicate-run guard and provenance-based idempotency for proposal execution
ideas.detail: Nothing prevents the same proposal from producing multiple runs rows (crash-retry, manual re-execution after status reset), which corrupts nearest-neighbor novelty (distance ~0 to its own twin). Cheapest path: before insert_run in execute_proposal, query runs for provenance_json proposal_id == this proposal and either reuse the row or refuse; longer-term add a UNIQUE index on (model_name, params_json, seed, origin) or a runs.proposal_id column with UNIQUE.

ideas.title: Extraction-era/schema-version column surfaced in coverage
ideas.detail: literature_methods records extracted_by_model but not which extraction prompt/schema era produced the row (single-label vs multi-label stylized_facts_targeted). The 'other'-suppression in build_coverage explicitly exists because eras differ, yet nothing lets coverage/gap_finder condition on or report the era mix. Cheapest path: add an extraction_schema_version INTEGER column stamped by update_literature_extraction, and include a per-era row count in build_coverage's summary dict and the heatmap title.

ideas.title: Cross-artifact consistency test suite
ideas.detail: The atlas publishes four views of the same corpus (coverage.png, coverage.md, gap-mine view A, literature_map.csv) and its own tests state they must not disagree — yet nothing checks it. Add tests that (1) parse render_markdown output back into a matrix and compare against cov['matrix']/totals cell-by-cell (would have caught the misaligned totals row), and (2) assert gap_finder view A column totals equal coverage col_totals when subfields are chosen to partition the corpus.

ideas.title: Single shared primary-tag selector in taxonomy
ideas.detail: coverage._primary_tag and literature_map.primary_tag claim to mirror each other but have fully diverged. Move the selection (normalise -> alias -> deny-lists -> concept fall-through) into taxonomy.primary_mechanism_tag(row) and make both modules thin wrappers, with a parametrised test feeding identical rows to both call sites. This is the same consolidation the module already did for CANONICAL_FACTS and would have prevented the drift.

ideas.title: Corpus-driven vocabulary drift report
ideas.detail: TAG_ALIASES and the family frozensets are curated by eyeballing heatmaps ('Yo's post-retag heatmap'). A small CLI that lists (a) mechanism tags landing in family 'other' ranked by paper count, (b) normalised slugs within edit-distance 1-2 of each other (e.g. 'minority-games' before its alias existed), and (c) tags hitting only substring heuristics rather than exact sets, would turn alias/family maintenance from anecdote into a routine, and quantify how much of the corpus each heuristic decides.

ideas.title: Deterministic atlas outputs (ordering + tie-breaking)
ideas.detail: load_literature has no ORDER BY, tfidf_matrix's df.most_common ties break by insertion order, and Counter.most_common in build_coverage ties break by first-seen — so row order, vocab membership at the max_features boundary, and top-N row selection can change between runs after unrelated DB churn, producing noisy diffs in the atlas_refresh PRs. Pin ORDER BY arxiv_id in load_literature and add explicit (count, name) sort keys; then the PNG/CSV/MD artifacts become reproducible and diffable.

ideas.title: Self-consistency test: every subfield/technique must be reachable by the gap joins
ideas.detail: None of the 30 tests would catch that 5/25 subfields can never match a paper or that 23/30 techniques can never join the corpus (one existing test even asserts the LOB cell is 0 without checking membership). Add a cheap invariant test: for each SUBFIELDS entry, `_matches_subfield` must return True for that subfield's own canonical mechanism slug(s); for each TECHNIQUES entry, at least one ref_papers id must be arxiv/OA-shaped (or be explicitly whitelisted as dashboard-only). This converts silent dead rows into CI failures.

ideas.title: Cross-view salience calibration + interleaving guarantee
ideas.detail: The ranking that feeds LLM proposals mixes a bounded [0,1.3] scale (view B) with an unbounded log scale (views A/C), so view B is starved. Normalise salience per view (percentile or z-score within view) before weighting, or guarantee representation by taking top-k per view and interleaving. Add a test that a >=5-sigma view-B deviation outranks a minimum-density (row=3,col=1) view-A gap.

ideas.title: DOI as a join key in literature_methods
ideas.detail: OpenAlex already returns DOIs, but literature_methods has no DOI column, which is the root cause of view C's 7/30 technique coverage and also blocks joining journal-only canon papers by their most stable identifier. Add a doi column on ingest, index _build_view_c's by_key by normalised DOI, and backfill via oa_paper_id lookups. This single change roughly quadruples view C's reachable surface.

ideas.title: Minimum-evidence guard for view B family deviations
ideas.detail: View B computes a family's median fingerprint from however many runs exist — a family with a single (possibly degenerate) run gets a 'structural blind spot' verdict, and a family with zero valid runs for a dim silently scores 0.0 ('perfectly reproduces the fact'). Record n_runs per (family, dim), require a floor (e.g. >=5 finite values) before scoring, and mark unmeasurable cells as NaN/'insufficient data' rather than 0 so the dashboard distinguishes 'fine' from 'unknown'.

ideas.title: Gap-view diagnostics page: per-row/column match counts and empty-context warnings
ideas.detail: Every major failure found here (dead subfields, DOI-orphan techniques, empty _scope_corpus_to_gap context, stripped references) is invisible in current output. Emit a diagnostics section alongside gap-mine: papers matched per subfield, refs resolved per technique, corpus size and reference count per generated proposal, with explicit WARNs when zero. A researcher would immediately see 'Limit order book: 0 papers matched' and distrust the atlas before acting on it.

ideas.title: Machine-readable atlas snapshot + coverage trend tracking
ideas.detail: build_atlas's rich per-subfield record (n_canon, n_in_db, fallback_used, error) is discarded after rendering one HTML page — there is no JSON artifact, so weekly runs cannot be diffed, coverage regressions (e.g. a subfield dropping from 80% to 0% because a query went noisy) are invisible, and none of the numbers are testable post-hoc. Cheapest path: `--json out.json` in cmd_canon_atlas dumping the atlas list verbatim, plus a tiny compare script the atlas_refresh PR body can embed ('coverage delta vs last run').

ideas.title: Ingestion provenance for canon rows
ideas.detail: Once --auto-ingest-missing runs, literature_methods has no record of WHY a paper is in the corpus (which subfield/query surfaced it, phrase vs seed-fallback, which run). This makes post-hoc corpus curation — e.g. purging papers ingested during a fallback outage — impossible. Cheapest path: an `ingest_provenance` TEXT column (or side table) written by ingest_by_ids/ingest_canon_via_oa with subfield key + method + timestamp.

ideas.title: Citation-walk cache and error accounting in genealogy
ideas.detail: Every genealogy render re-queries OpenAlex (~21+ calls for depth 2), results are non-reproducible across runs, and failed lookups are indistinguishable from leaves. A per-work JSON cache (keyed by W-id, with fetched_at) would make trees reproducible, allow re-rendering with different keyword filters for free, and a `failed_lookups` list in the tree dict would let render_html show an 'incomplete' banner. This is the single cheapest fix for the silent-truncation hazard.

ideas.title: Canon sanity assertions (seed-in-canon, fallback-drift alarm)
ideas.detail: There is no automatic check that a subfield's own seed paper appears in its detected canon (it should, by construction, be among the top-cited), nor that fallback canon overlaps topically with the last known-good phrase canon. Both are cheap invariants: assert seed W-id ∈ papers (warn otherwise — catches query drift and OpenAlex reindexing), and Jaccard-compare fallback canon against the previous snapshot's canon for the same subfield, flagging <20% overlap as 'fallback returned a different literature'.

ideas.title: Rendering-layer regression tests for the aggregate numbers
ideas.detail: Tests cover build_atlas/missing_canon/filter_tree logic but nothing asserts the HTML's aggregate claims: a property test that the rendered overall coverage is ≤100% and equals total_in_db/total_canon would have caught the 300% subtitle bug, and a golden test with fallback_used=True entries would have exposed that the badge is never rendered. Both are pure-function tests on render_html output, ~50 lines total.

ideas.title: Sync test binding CANONICAL_FACTS ↔ i18n ↔ glossary
ideas.detail: Nothing asserts that every CANONICAL_FACTS slug resolves to a real glossary entry (vs. silently falling back) and that the i18n maps contain no dead keys. A ~15-line pytest — for f in CANONICAL_FACTS: assert f=='other' or _glossary_entry(f) or f in _FACT_FALLBACK; assert set(_FACT_TO_GLOSSARY_EN) <= set(CANONICAL_FACTS); and assert i18n label == glossary ja_primary when the glossary has the term — would have caught both the gain-loss-asymmetry drift and the herding leftover, and will catch every future enum edit.

ideas.title: Freshness/provenance stamp on every dashboard page and copied artifact
ideas.detail: The dashboard is a 'static snapshot' but records nothing about when or from what it was built: no generation timestamp, no DB row count/hash, no source mtime next to each copied PNG or the canon atlas. A researcher cannot tell whether index.html reflects yesterday's corpus or last month's, and the canon-shadowing bug is invisible precisely because of this. Cheapest path: embed build time, db_path, len(rows), and per-asset 'source: <path> (mtime)' into the footer/status div in build_dashboard, plus a visible 'stale?' badge when the copied canon atlas is older than the DB's newest literature row.

ideas.title: Hostile-input golden test for all HTML generators
ideas.detail: dashboard.py is XSS-safe today only because DB free-text (titles/abstracts/tags) happens never to be interpolated into its pages — the discipline is accidental, not enforced. A shared fixture corpus with titles/abstracts/mechanism_tags containing <script>, quotes, and unicode (fullwidth parens, combining chars) run through dashboard, canon_atlas, genealogy, and literature_map render paths, asserting the raw payload never appears unescaped, would protect against the first person who adds a 'recent papers' panel to the dashboard (an obvious future feature).

ideas.title: --strict mode for the dashboard CLI so CI can't ship silently degraded pages
ideas.detail: Section failures (coverage render exception, gap detection exception, missing figures) all degrade to inline empty-state divs with exit code 0, so the atlas_refresh workflow will happily commit a dashboard whose coverage matrix or gap table vanished. Add a strict flag (default on in CI) that collects per-section errors during build_dashboard and returns non-zero / prints them to stderr, so a matplotlib or gap_finder regression fails the workflow instead of shipping a hollow dashboard.

ideas.title: Round-trip check that lookfor prose matches code constants
ideas.detail: The 'What to look for' blocks hand-duplicate quantitative facts from other modules (feature list and dimensionality from fingerprint.FEATURE_NAMES, distance definition from inverse_abm, ZI role from abm_families) — the 6-vs-9 drift is the first symptom. Cheapest fix: interpolate the constants into the strings (f'{len(FEATURE_NAMES)}-dim', ', '.join(FEATURE_NAMES)) instead of hand-writing them; where prose must stay prose, add a test asserting the rendered markets.html mentions the current dimensionality and every FEATURE_NAMES component alias exactly once.

ideas.title: Post-scaffold compile/import/protocol validation gate
ideas.detail: The idea_plan module docstring (lines 18-21) already promises 'on syntax error / import error / non-ABMModel-conformance, the scaffold is saved anyway and the failure is logged on the idea's row' — but no such code exists anywhere. Cheapest path: after writing the files, run py_compile.compile(path, doraise=True), importlib.import_module on the generated module, and an ABMModel-protocol isinstance check, then persist the result (ok/error string) into a new scaffold_check field via update_idea. This turns three of the confirmed silent-failure modes into visible, recorded ones.

ideas.title: `judge --id` (re-judge existing idea rows)
ideas.detail: There is no way to attach or refresh a judgment on an existing idea — which breaks promote-proposal outright and also prevents a rigorous workflow the corpus growth invites: re-judging an old idea after 50 new papers are ingested to see whether its novelty verdict decays. Cheapest path: add an --id alternative to --idea/--idea-file in cmd_judge that loads idea_text from the row and calls update_idea instead of insert_idea, preserving proposal_ids provenance.

ideas.title: Shared LLM-output schema validator for judge and plan
ideas.detail: llm_client only guarantees 'a dict'; every downstream field (category enum, closest_literature_arxiv_ids list-of-str, confidence range, param values vs MODEL_BOUNDS ±30%, references subset of candidates) is trusted raw. A single validate(verdict_schema, obj) helper with per-field coercion/flagging — applied in judge_idea and make_plan, with violations stored as warnings like the existing arxiv filter — would close the whole class of confirmed type-fragility and silent-misrouting defects, and could feed a retry ('your JSON violated X, re-emit') loop that llm_client's transient-retry machinery almost already supports.

ideas.title: CLI-layer tests with dry-run hooks
ideas.detail: tests/test_idea.py covers the library functions well but never invokes any cmd_* handler; the promote→plan dead-end, the cmd_list crashes, the run exit-code gap, and the warnings-drop divergence all live exclusively in idea_cli.py's untested glue. Cheapest path: a test that builds args via the real argparse parser and calls handlers with judge/plan monkeypatched (the dry_run_* hooks already exist), asserting on DB state and exit codes — including the full promote-proposal → plan sequence that currently fails.

ideas.title: Idea-pipeline provenance: raw LLM responses, token usage, and prompt versions on the idea row
ideas.detail: The ideas table stores only the parsed verdict/plan and the model name. When a verdict looks wrong months later, there is no way to distinguish LLM error from post-filter mutation (_filter_verdict_arxiv_ids mutates the verdict in place before persistence) or from a prompt change (PLAN_SYSTEM_PROMPT/VERDICT_SYSTEM_PROMPT are unversioned). Cheapest path: persist raw_response_json, a hash of the system prompt, and usage tokens (both SDKs return them) alongside judgment_json/plan_json — near-zero cost, large payoff for auditing a research pipeline whose outputs feed publications.

ideas.title: Bounds + runtime-budget gate before (auto-)execution
ideas.detail: The prompt promises params within [low, high]±30% but nothing enforces it, and cmd_auto executes fresh 'proposed' rows unattended (the 'manual review gate' of approve is bypassed by design). Missing: a pre-execute gate that (a) rejects/clips params outside bounds*1.3, (b) caps size-like params (T, n_steps, N) at a configurable budget, and (c) wraps model.run in a wall-clock timeout. Cheapest path: extend _validate_proposal with a numeric bounds check (it already has MODEL_BOUNDS in scope) and add a signal/timeout wrapper in execute_proposal.

ideas.title: Single NaN-row policy via db.collect_population everywhere
ideas.detail: db.collect_population() already returns (fps, kept_rows) aligned, but execute_proposal, propose_next_k, _per_family_centroids and _describe_sparse_regions each roll their own (inconsistent) NaN handling — which produced the index-misalignment crash and the NaN-first UCB ranking. Replace all ad-hoc filtering with collect_population and add a regression test that seeds one NaN-fingerprint run before exercising every consumer.

ideas.title: Integration test that calls execute_proposal itself, with adversarial fixtures
ideas.detail: tests/test_propose.py 'end-to-end execute' test re-implements the execute steps inline (insert_run + update_proposal_status by hand) instead of calling execute_proposal, so none of the three empirically-confirmed bugs (NaN IndexError, None-format crash, orphan-run duplication) were catchable. Cheapest path: a test that monkeypatches build_model/series_for_fingerprint (as the repro scripts here do) and runs execute_proposal + the cmd_auto loop against DBs containing (a) a NaN run, (b) a proposal without predicted_fingerprint, (c) a repeat execution after simulated crash.

ideas.title: Pin real-market replays to stored timestamps and persist raw series
ideas.detail: populate_real already stores first_ts/last_ts/window_start_idx in params, but label_cli replays by re-fetching 'years back from now' with the cache disabled, so the labelled plot drifts from the fingerprinted window. Cheapest path: add period1/period2 arguments to fetch_yahoo_closes and slice by timestamp; better: store a content hash (or the return series itself, ~4KB gzipped) at ingest so replay is exact and offline.

ideas.title: Comparability of prediction_error across time (frozen standardisation + multi-seed)
ideas.detail: prediction_error is an L2 in a standardised space whose mu/sd are recomputed from the ever-growing run population at each execution, so the 'LLM learning curve' plot conflates model improvement with atlas drift; and each fingerprint is a single-seed draw, so error includes irreducible seed noise of unknown size. Cheapest path: store the (mu, sd) snapshot used per execution (or recompute all historical errors under current stats when plotting), and execute each proposal at 3 seeds to report mean fingerprint ± dispersion — this also quantifies which part of prediction_error the LLM could ever remove.

ideas.title: Masked distance + per-feature validity instead of fabricated values
ideas.detail: Two of the top findings (hill=20 fabrication, nansum false proximity) share a root cause: the pipeline has no way to say 'this feature is undefined for this run'. Add a validity mask (store NaN in fingerprint_json, keep rows) and compute distances as sqrt(d/k * sum over the k shared finite features). This removes the structural anti-GCMG/MG bias in inverse-ABM and makes distance_matrix NaN-safe by construction. Cheapest path: one masked-distance helper in fingerprint.py + swap the three call sites.

ideas.title: Feature-range gate: assert every stored fingerprint lies in its documented range
ideas.detail: The agg_kurt_decay blow-up (+21.4 stored vs documented ~[-2,+1]) sat unnoticed in the production DB. Declare an expected range per FEATURE_NAMES entry and have populate/validation_gate flag any row outside it (count in the gate JSON, warning in the populate log). Hours of work; would have caught both the ratio instability and the GCMG series contamination (vol 60 vs MG's ~8-10) immediately.

ideas.title: Adapter-vs-model contract tests and golden fingerprint fixtures
ideas.detail: No test asserts that series_for_fingerprint returns the model-native excess (assert np.array_equal(series, result['excess']) for GCMG, 2*A-N for MG) — the exact gap that let the GCMG fallback bug ship. Add that plus 2-3 pinned (seeded series -> expected 9-vector) golden fixtures so any silent change to feature math or FEATURE_NAMES ordering fails loudly instead of drifting the DB geometry between populate runs.

ideas.title: Fingerprint noise floor: seed-replicate variance in the validation gate
ideas.detail: The gate reports inter/intra separation but never asks whether separation exceeds pure seed noise: run each (model, params) at 3-5 seeds, report mean within-params distance as the measurement-noise floor, and require mean_inter >> noise floor (an effect-size, not just ratio > 1). Without it, a 'passing' gate can reflect LHS parameter spread rather than genuine family separation, and silhouette values are uninterpretable against sampling error.

ideas.title: Fingerprint schema versioning in the runs table
ideas.detail: runs.fingerprint_json has no feature-version column; a DB populated pre-v4 (6 features) mixed with v4 rows makes collect_population's np.vstack either crash or silently misalign axes (feature 4 means different things). Add a fingerprint_version column (or store feature_names in provenance) and have load_runs/collect_population validate length == len(FEATURE_NAMES), skipping-with-warning on mismatch. Current DB is uniformly length 9, so migration is a no-op today — the cheap moment to do it.

ideas.title: Escape-aware round-trip property test for the edit-file format
ideas.detail: The render/parse pair (_render_edit_file/_parse_edit_file) is the load-bearing serialization for the user's irreplaceable commentary, yet nothing guarantees render(parse(x)) stability when bodies contain section-header-like lines ('## word'), duplicate headers, CRLF, or literal '\n## tags\n' strings — three corruption modes were demonstrated empirically. Add an escaping rule (prefix body lines matching the header regex on render, unescape on parse) plus a hypothesis/fuzz property test that round-trips arbitrary multi-line unicode bodies through render→parse→render.

ideas.title: import-md safety gate: header/name match, minimum-section check, and clear-diff preview
ideas.detail: import-md is invoked in a shell for-loop over PR files and writes whatever it parses, including five empty strings from a wrong or malformed file. Cheap fix: refuse when zero recognized sections are found, verify the file's '# methodology notes for: <name>' line matches the CLI name argument, and print a before/after diff (like cmd_edit does) with a --yes/--allow-clear flag required to blank previously non-empty columns.

ideas.title: Validate extracted code URLs before persisting them
ideas.detail: code_links stores regex-derived URLs (with demonstrated truncation, subdomain, prose-weld, and base-model false positives) straight into literature_methods, and downstream snapshot/idea-plan stages then trust them. A single HEAD request to api.github.com/repos/<org>/<repo> (already rate-limit-aware via GITHUB_TOKEN) would classify each URL as exists/404/redirected at extraction time; store that status and never feed unverified or 404 URLs into LLM context. This also turns the 'errs toward 404' design claim into a measured property.

ideas.title: Replace the dead PWC fallback and add a network-source canary
ideas.detail: paperswithcode.com now 302s to huggingface.co, so the third code-url source silently returns None forever and 'pwc=0' looks like normal scarcity. Swap in a living source (OpenAlex primary_location / Semantic Scholar externalIds, both already integrated elsewhere in the package) and add a tiny scheduled CI job that queries one known-good ID per external service (arxiv, S2, OpenAlex, GitHub raw) and fails loudly on redirects/HTML — the codebase's swallow-everything HTTP helpers make dead services otherwise invisible.

ideas.title: Audit trail / undo for methods commentary
ideas.detail: update_method destructively overwrites the columns the package itself describes as 'genuinely hard to copy because it accumulates one careful read at a time', and four separate code paths (edit, draft --apply, draft-all, import-md) can clear them with no history. Add a methods_history table (name, column, old_value, new_value, source, timestamp) written inside the same transaction as every UPDATE, plus a 'methods undo <name>' subcommand. Hours of work, and it converts every wipe bug in this audit from data loss into an inconvenience.

ideas.title: Nightly citation-identity check for all curated ids
ideas.detail: The package already has a citation validator for LLM output, yet three curated ids (cond-mat/9810262, 1502.03003, 1903.04279) are confirmed wrong papers and one shipped bad id was only half-fixed. Missing: a job that resolves every subfields.seed_arxiv, techniques.ref_papers, and abm_families.arxiv_id via arXiv/OpenAlex and fuzzy-matches the returned title/authors against the entry's name/notes, failing CI on mismatch. Cheapest path: reuse openalex.fetch_paper + a difflib ratio threshold; run in atlas_refresh.yml where OpenAlex is already contacted.

ideas.title: Wire the existing validators into a real execution path
ideas.detail: validate_subfields()/validate_techniques() exist but are called by nothing (the docstring even claims canon_atlas calls one). Add: (a) a pytest asserting both return clean, (b) a stderr warning at the top of the canon-atlas and gap-mine CLIs, and (c) extend validation to structural cross-checks the current code can't express: ABM_FAMILIES keys ⊆ REGISTRY ∪ {documented-pending}, ABM_FAMILY_JA keys == ABM_FAMILIES keys, your_impl paths exist on disk.

ideas.title: Generate fidelity notes from code instead of prose
ideas.detail: Five of nine family cards misdescribe the registry implementations (ZI/CI/FW/GCMG/LM). The 'honest caveat' layer should be at least partly mechanical: introspect each REGISTRY class for its actual constructor/dataclass parameters and the reference cited in its own module docstring, render that alongside the curated prose on the dashboard card, and fail a test when a fidelity note mentions a parameter name (β, ε, cancellation rate) that doesn't appear in the model's signature or MODEL_BOUNDS.

ideas.title: DOI join key for gap view C
ideas.detail: Techniques citing pre-arxiv classics by DOI (Hill 1975, Pickands 1975, Politis-Romano 1994, PAMS Jxiv) can never register literature overlap because literature_methods has no doi column and gap_finder joins only on arxiv/W-ids. Add a doi column populated by canon_ingest/populate_refs, normalise DOIs (lowercase, strip https://doi.org/) on both sides, and include them in by_key — otherwise view C 'gaps' for validation/tail-stats techniques are permanent artefacts.

ideas.title: Glossary-compliance lint over user-facing Japanese strings
ideas.detail: The banned coinage 富動的 sits in i18n.py despite the glossary explicitly rejecting the 富動 family — proving prompt-side enforcement doesn't police the codebase's own strings. Add a test that scans i18n.py, dashboard templates, and prompt scaffolds for every glossary avoid[].bad term plus the 動学 rule, and (cheaply) also asserts every ABM_FAMILY_JA gloss stays consistent with the corresponding abm_families mechanism text to prevent bilingual drift.

ideas.title: Pin the extraction prompt to the taxonomy (single-source the fact enum)
ideas.detail: The 10-fact vocabulary lives in taxonomy.CANONICAL_FACTS and, duplicated, inside EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT's comment block; build_coverage silently drops non-canonical facts, so drift is invisible. Cheapest guard: a test asserting each CANONICAL_FACTS slug appears quoted in the prompt and vice versa (the unused EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT import in test_arxiv_ingest.py:13 suggests this was intended). Better: generate the prompt's fact list from taxonomy at import time.

ideas.title: Real-executor round-trip test with degenerate fingerprints
ideas.detail: No test calls propose_cli.execute_proposal or any analytics.* function; prediction_error — the metric the whole propose->execute->learning-curve loop optimizes — is computed by unexecuted code (and currently crashes when any DB run has a NaN fingerprint). Cheapest path: temp DB with 2 good runs + 1 all-NaN run, insert a small cont_bouchaud param_sweep proposal, call execute_proposal, hand-compute the expected standardized L2, then call analytics.summarize/plot_* on the same DB and assert PNGs + stats.

ideas.title: Subcommand smoke sweep for the three CLIs
ideas.detail: arxiv_cli has 25+ subcommands with ~5 tested; idea_cli, propose_cli, inverse_abm_cli, label_cli are essentially untested glue where NameError-class rot hides (one instance proven at arxiv_cli.py:1349). Cheapest path: a parametrized test that builds a tiny seeded temp DB once, then invokes every cmd_* handler with dry-run/list-style args and asserts rc in {0,1,2} with no traceback. This catches missing imports, argparse drift, and schema mismatches for a few dozen lines of test code.

ideas.title: CI hardening: un-neuter lint, add coverage floor, run on working branches
ideas.detail: Remove '|| true' (hard-fail at least on ruff --select F,E9 which today catches a real crash), add pytest-cov with a per-package floor so the nine zero-coverage modules become visible in every PR, and extend push triggers to the long-lived working branches so 89-commit branches don't accumulate unverified. All three are single-line workflow edits plus one dev-dependency.

ideas.title: Golden literature-corpus fixture exercising the real load_literature contract end-to-end
ideas.detail: Every consumer (coverage, gap_finder, dashboard, literature_map, gap_propose) parses literature rows slightly differently, and tests feed each one hand-rolled dicts (often the legacy comma-string form). Missing: one frozen ~20-row corpus JSON containing multi-label facts, 'other'-only rows, JA/unicode tags, versioned and oa:W-synthetic ids, NULL abstracts — inserted via the real upsert/update APIs, read back via load_literature, and pushed through build_coverage + find_gaps + build_dashboard in a single test. This converts fixture-realism drift from a per-module hazard into one assertable contract.

ideas.title: Snapshot round-trip invariance test
ideas.detail: Nothing verifies that dump(restore(snapshot)) == snapshot, which is exactly the bug that wipes code_url/source_kind/s2/oa columns weekly. Add a pytest that builds a temp SQLite with every column populated (including an 'oa:W123' canon row and user_notes), runs the exact restore code the workflows embed, dumps via load_literature, and asserts deep equality. Extract the inline heredoc Python (duplicated verbatim in 3 workflows) into `fingerprint_atlas.snapshot restore/dump` CLI so the test covers the real code path.

ideas.title: Workflow smoke-test job in CI
ideas.detail: The workflows reference 20+ CLI flags across 6 entry points that were heavily refactored; nothing catches drift until a cron fails silently on a weekend. Add a CI job that runs each workflow's command sequence with --dry-run/tiny params and no network (populate --n-per-model 1, ingest with a fixtures `papers` list, dump-md, queue-next, atlas with a seeded DB), or run the YAML under `act`. Also assert the create-pull-request add-paths globs actually match non-gitignored generated files (would have caught canon_atlas.html).

ideas.title: Concurrency groups + mergeable snapshot format
ideas.detail: Add `concurrency:` groups to the four cron workflows and replace the single monolithic literature_methods.json with per-paper files (data/literature/<arxiv_id>.json) or sorted JSONL keyed by arxiv_id, so out-of-order or overlapping ingest PRs merge additively instead of clobbering. Also have create-pull-request reuse a fixed branch (e.g. ingest-arxiv/weekly) so a new run updates the open PR rather than stacking stale ones.

ideas.title: Partial-progress fences on network-flaky steps
ideas.detail: Weekly runs currently discard all completed work when any of Yahoo, OpenAlex, Groq/OpenAI or a single paper extraction fails. Add `if: always()` on the snapshot dump + PR steps, `continue-on-error` on canon-atlas and populate_refs real-data pass (or --skip-real in CI with committed cache fixtures), pass OPENALEX_API_KEY to atlas_refresh, and surface partial failure in the PR body instead of failing the job. This converts hard weekly outages into reviewable partial artifacts.

ideas.title: Extraction contract enforcement + provenance stamping
ideas.detail: The taxonomy prompt enum is enforced nowhere in code: validate stylized_facts_targeted ⊆ CANONICAL_FACTS and tags against a slug charset at extract_paper_structured, reject/park rows that fail, and stamp each extraction with a prompt-version hash in the DB/snapshot so retag campaigns (extract-untagged --retag-all-extracted) can target exactly the rows produced under an old contract. This also shrinks the prompt-injection surface (arbitrary attacker strings can no longer enter committed matrices and dashboards).

ideas.title: Prompt-vs-taxonomy sync test
ideas.detail: The stylized-fact enum is hand-copied into two LLM prompts (arxiv_ingest.EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT, idea_judge.ASPECT_SYSTEM_PROMPT). They are in sync today only by discipline — the herding removal had to be manually propagated. A ~20-line test that regex-extracts the quoted fact slugs from each prompt string and asserts set-equality with taxonomy.CANONICAL_FACTS would make future vocabulary edits self-checking. Same trick for the 9 predicted_fingerprint keys hard-coded in propose.SYSTEM_PROMPT vs fingerprint.FEATURE_NAMES.

ideas.title: FACT_ALIASES + ingest-time enum validation with rejects log
ideas.detail: Mechanism tags get TAG_ALIASES + deny-lists; facts get nothing — any LLM label drift ('volatility-clustering', 'heavy-tails') persists to the DB and silently vanishes from every matrix. Add a small FACT_ALIASES dict in taxonomy, validate extracted facts at extract_paper_structured time (alias → canonical, else coerce to 'other' and record the original), and a CLI report of non-canonical fact labels currently in the DB. Cheapest guard against systematic coverage deflation when the extraction model changes.

ideas.title: Fingerprint probes for gain-loss-asymmetry (and a volume story)
ideas.detail: Two of the ten canonical facts (gain-loss-asymmetry, volume-volatility-corr) are invisible to view B and to inverse-ABM matching because no FEATURE_NAMES dim measures them. Gain-loss asymmetry is return-only measurable (time-reversal asymmetry statistic, or drawdown/drawup first-passage time ratio a la Jensen-Johansen-Simonsen) — a v5 feature would restore a full Cont-fact axis to the family-vs-real-market analysis. Volume-vol-corr needs a volume series; at minimum, document per-adapter whether volume exists and emit the correlation where it does.

ideas.title: Per-view salience calibration (or quota) in rank_top_gaps
ideas.detail: Views A/C use log-density salience (typically 2-4) while view B is max-normalised to 1.0, so weights cannot make them commensurable and view B is starved out of the top-N (verified: a 5-sigma blind spot ranks below every routine empty cell). Percentile-rank salience within each view before weighting, or reserve a per-view quota in the top-N, and add a test with realistic (non-equal) salience magnitudes rather than the current equal-salience fixture.

ideas.title: Subfield-matcher round-trip audit
ideas.detail: gap_finder reuses subfields.title_any (designed for raw-title matching in canon_atlas) against a hyphen-normalised tag haystack, and nothing checks the two conventions agree — four subfields silently match nothing. Add (a) a lint/test asserting every title_any token, after _norm_tag, can match its own normalised form, and (b) a diagnostic CLI that prints, per subfield, how many corpus papers it captures and the top tags driving the match — which would also expose over-broad single-word tokens like 'market' and 'return' that currently vacuum unrelated papers into behavioral subfields.

ideas.title: Bootstrap-over-papers stability layer for gap ranking
ideas.detail: The top-15/top-5 gap lists drive LLM proposals and (via execution) compute spend, yet a single added paper can reshuffle them. Missing: any stability measure. Cheapest path: in find_gaps, resample the rows list with replacement B=200 times, rerun build_views + rank_top_gaps (pure NumPy, seconds at ~200 papers), and attach per-gap selection frequency and rank quantiles to each Gap; dashboard shows 'appears in 87% of resamples' next to salience and greys out <60%. This simultaneously provides an implicit multiple-comparison guard for the ~1,000 screened cells.

ideas.title: Extraction-instrument reliability audit (prompt-version stamps + kappa)
ideas.detail: The entire coverage/gap edifice rests on one LLM pass per abstract with no reliability estimate and no record of which prompt produced which row. Add extraction_prompt_version to literature_methods (stamped in update_literature_extraction); then re-extract a fixed random subsample of ~30 papers twice (same prompt/different seeds, and old vs new prompt) and report per-fact-column Cohen's kappa and per-column recall shift. Without this, no referee can weigh an 'empty leverage cell' against extraction noise — the project already knows the old prompt undercounted leverage but has never quantified by how much.

ideas.title: Row-normalised + independence-residual coverage companion views
ideas.detail: The external reviewer's demand (commit 9ca0395) is still open. Two cheap figures from the existing cov dict: (1) share view M[i,j]/row_papers[i]; (2) Poisson/chi-square residual view (observed - row*col/N)/sqrt(row*col/N), which doubles as the corrected gap salience. Render beside the absolute heatmap in dashboard.research; both are <50 lines reusing render_heatmap.

ideas.title: Per-market view B baselines with minimum-data gating
ideas.detail: View B currently pools SPX+BTC (and overlapping 'full' windows) into one median/std, silently falls back to std=1.0, and codes missing measurements as zero deviation. Restructure: compute deviation per real market from non-overlapping windows only (min over markets = 'family matches at least one real market'), require >=4 finite baseline values per dim (else NaN + explicit skip note in the view description), and use ddof=1. Also record n_runs per family cell so a 1-run family's median is not read as the family's behaviour.

ideas.title: Curated-taxonomy matching validation harness
ideas.detail: Nothing verifies that subfields/techniques/families actually connect to the corpus: 5/25 subfields are provably unreachable (space-vs-hyphen), techniques join on tiny hand-typed ref_paper id lists, and abm_families join on exact model_name keys. Add a CI check that (a) every SUBFIELDS title_any needle survives _norm_tag round-trip matching, (b) every subfield/technique matches >=1 row of the live DB (warn listing the dead ones), (c) every ABM_FAMILIES key exists in the runs registry. This converts silent axis shrinkage into a visible failure.

ideas.title: Central schema+enum validator for every LLM response (Pydantic/jsonschema)
ideas.detail: Each call site re-implements ad-hoc coercion (extract_paper_structured, _validate_proposal, propose_from_gap, _filter_verdict_arxiv_ids) and they disagree: some check enums, some check presence, some check nothing. A single validate(response, schema, enums=taxonomy.CANONICAL_FACTS) layer applied inside llm_client (or a thin wrapper) would close the liquidity/sparsity, JA-tag, null-fingerprint, and null-category holes at once and keep the prompt's promised schema and the code's accepted schema in lockstep. Effort: day.

ideas.title: Contract tests that parse the prompt's JSON schema and assert the validator enforces it
ideas.detail: For each prompt, extract the documented output keys+enums and assert (a) every promised field is validated and (b) values outside the enum are rejected — a regression net for the exact 'prompt promises X, validator never checks X' drift found here (references mandatory-but-unchecked, facts enum, fingerprint float-ness). Cheap because prompts are module constants. Effort: day.

ideas.title: Fuzz/property tests feeding adversarial LLM payloads through each pipeline
ideas.detail: Drive extract_paper_structured, propose_from_corpus (dry_run_payload), judge_idea (dry_run_*), make_plan (dry_run_response) and scaffold with malformed dicts: null enum values, non-ASCII, code-injection class names, empty lists, wrong types. Today these paths are only tested on the happy path, which is why the null-category and null-fingerprint crashes survived. Effort: day.

ideas.title: Idempotent DB write-guards + orphan-run reconciliation
ideas.detail: execute_proposal can insert a run then fail before linking it, and re-execution duplicates runs. A pre-check that a proposal already has executed_run_id, plus a periodic reconcile that flags runs with provenance.source=='propose_execute' whose proposal_id is unlinked, would prevent silent runs-table drift that biases standardize()/novelty. Effort: hours.

ideas.title: Model/temperature/retry policy registry instead of per-module constants
ideas.detail: DEFAULT_*_MODEL, temperature, and generate_japanese are duplicated across 7 modules and CI defaults to a different provider (gpt-4o-mini) than code (gpt-oss-120b). A single policy table keyed by task ('extraction'->EN/temp0.3, 'gap'->JA/temp0.6, ...) would make the generate_japanese-per-consumer correctness auditable in one place and stop drift like extract_aspects=JA. Effort: hours.

ideas.title: Cross-source identity resolution (DOI/title dedupe) for literature_methods
ideas.detail: The single biggest data-integrity gap: papers are identified only by exact arxiv_id string, and the arxiv/OpenAlex dual-ingest design makes duplicate physical papers likely (preprint vs journal OA works, missing OA arxiv linkage). Add a DOI column (fetch_work_full and arxiv already expose it), a normalized (title, year) index, a pre-insert duplicate check in upsert_literature_metadata/ingest_canon_via_oa, and a `find-duplicates` / `merge-rows` CLI that consolidates extraction + enrichment + snapshots + user_notes. Cheapest path: one SELECT on lower(trim(title)) before minting an 'oa:' id.

ideas.title: Enforced state machines + transactional lifecycle writes
ideas.detail: proposals (proposed→approved/rejected→executed) and ideas (judged→planned→scaffolded→executed) have zero transition enforcement; execute is not atomic (run insert and status update are separate connections/commits). Centralize transitions in db.py with an allowed-transitions table, and make execute_proposal write insert_run + update_proposal_status inside one sqlite transaction so a crash can never leave an orphan run. Add a UNIQUE partial index on proposals.executed_run_id.

ideas.title: `db doctor` integrity command
ideas.detail: Nothing today can detect the orphan states this audit found: runs whose provenance_json cites a proposal that is not 'executed' or whose executed_run_id points elsewhere, literature_code_snapshots rows without a matching literature row, ideas with status ahead of their populated columns (judged w/o judgment_json), proposals.references_json citing deleted papers, duplicate (model_name,params_json,seed) run rows, and vN-suffixed arxiv_ids. A read-only doctor subcommand printing each class of anomaly (plus --fix for the safe ones) would make every future migration verifiable in seconds.

ideas.title: Extraction provenance/versioning (prompt hash + schema version per row)
ideas.detail: The extraction prompt has changed contract at least twice (single→multi-label facts, JA-leak fix), and the only recovery is the blunt --retag-all-extracted. Store a prompt_version (hash of EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT) and extracted_at alongside extracted_by_model; then 'retag rows extracted under prompt < N' becomes a targeted, resumable query, and the coverage matrix can annotate which cells mix vocabularies. Hours of work: one column + one WHERE clause in extract-untagged.

ideas.title: Idempotence keys for runs (content hash of model+params+seed)
ideas.detail: populate, propose execute, synthetic/real injectors all append to runs with no uniqueness; duplicates silently corrupt novelty distances (min-NN distance → 0), sparse-region detection, and preference sampling. Add a generated UNIQUE column hash(model_name, params_json, seed, origin, series_kind) with INSERT OR IGNORE semantics plus a one-shot dedup migration; re-running any populate step then becomes safely idempotent, which the CI workflows implicitly assume today.

ideas.title: Emergency corpus rescue + committed snapshot with row-count ratchet
ideas.detail: The retagged ~120-paper corpus quoted in commit 11e9d59 exists only in some unlocated local DB. Find it (search other machines/containers for abm_knowhow.db or data/abm_knowhow.db), export via load_literature() to data/literature_methods.json, commit it, and add a CI step that fails if the restored snapshot's row count drops below the last committed count. Cheapest possible insurance against the total-loss state this audit found.

ideas.title: Full-fidelity snapshot round-trip test
ideas.detail: My temp-DB repro shows the dump->restore loop loses source_kind, oa_*, s2_*, user_notes, user_tags, code_url. Add a pytest that builds a row with every column populated, runs the exact workflow dump+materialise code, and asserts load_literature(db1) == load_literature(db2) field-by-field. This turns the currently silent corruption into a red test.

ideas.title: Executable DB data-quality gate (the checks this audit had to run by hand)
ideas.detail: Ship a `arxiv_cli --db X doctor` command that runs the exact hygiene battery this audit was assigned: stylized_facts_targeted values outside the enum, mechanism_tags case-variant duplicates / non-ASCII / comma corruption, arxiv_id vN suffixes and oa:W-vs-source_kind consistency, relevance_score<threshold survivors, extraction_attempts parking, empty abstracts, and orphan proposal/idea references. Run it in atlas_refresh before rendering so bad data can never silently reach the published matrices.

ideas.title: Label provenance and simulated-label quarantine
ideas.detail: runs.preference_label cannot distinguish the 25 scripted acceptance-test labels from real human taste. Add a labeled_by column ('human'|'simulation'|'import'), have the self-label simulation tag itself, and make the preference model exclude non-human labels by default. Also surface 'all labels written within N ms' as a warning in label_cli summary.

ideas.title: Durable proposals/ideas persistence + dashboard artifact tracking
ideas.detail: Proposals and ideas live only in per-run ephemeral DBs (propose.yml rebuilds data/abm_knowhow.db each run and dumps markdown, not rows), so prediction_error learning curves can never accumulate and the committed PNGs are orphaned. Persist proposals/ideas to a committed JSON snapshot exactly like literature (same restore step), and either track dashboard/assets in git or publish them as release/workflow artifacts so figures remain tied to the data+commit that produced them.

ideas.title: Snapshot round-trip contract test (dump → restore → dump losslessness)
ideas.detail: The literature snapshot JSON is the only persisted state the three cron workflows share, yet nothing tests that restoring it and re-dumping preserves every column (it demonstrably does not — oa_*/s2_*/code_url/source_kind are lost). A pure-SQLite test that builds a fully-populated row, runs the exact restore code the workflows embed, and asserts dict equality would have caught the data-loss bug and will pin the contract as columns are added. Cheapest path: factor the workflows' inline heredoc Python into fingerprint_atlas.db.restore_literature_snapshot() so it is importable and testable, then call that from all three YAMLs.

ideas.title: Parametrized CLI smoke-test layer for the automation entry points
ideas.detail: arxiv_cli's ~34 subcommands, propose_cli, idea_cli, inverse_abm_cli, populate, populate_refs and label_cli main()/argparse dispatch are 0-17% covered, yet they are exactly what the cron workflows execute. A single parametrized test that invokes each subcommand's cmd_* with a tmp seeded DB (network + call_llm monkeypatched, --dry-run where available) plus a `--help` sweep over main() would lift the 0% modules cheaply and catch argparse-wiring regressions before the Friday/Monday crons do.

ideas.title: Coverage measurement in CI with per-module floors
ideas.detail: pytest-cov is not even in the dev extras; nobody has been seeing the 47% number. Add pytest-cov to [project.optional-dependencies].dev, run `--cov=packages/fingerprint_atlas --cov-report=term` in ci.yml, and set fail_under floors for the modules the workflows depend on (db, propose, gap_propose, arxiv_ingest already at 85-95% — freeze them there) so coverage can only ratchet up.

ideas.title: Workflow rehearsal job in PR CI
ideas.detail: The 4 automation workflows only ever execute on cron against main, so bit-rot (empty-DB exit-1 in atlas step, Yahoo dependency, snapshot restore drift) is discovered at 23:00 Friday instead of at review time. Add a ci.yml job that runs the same command sequence as atlas_refresh/propose with --skip-real, a committed fixture snapshot, and dry-run LLM flags; assert the expected artifacts exist. This converts three of this audit's findings into red PR checks.

ideas.title: Adversarial LLM-output fuzz tests for the extraction/proposal validators
ideas.detail: The validators (extract_paper_structured coercions, propose_from_gap param/fingerprint gating, _normalize_json_response, judge_idea hallucination filter) are only tested with well-formed fakes plus a handful of known-bad shapes. A small property/table-driven suite feeding realistic LLM pathologies — nested lists, numeric strings, NaN/inf relevance, mixed-language tags, 100k-char summaries, duplicate keys, markdown-fenced JSON — would harden the ingestion path that writes directly into the research corpus, where a silently mis-coerced relevance_score biases every downstream coverage/gap analysis.

ideas.title: Fact-alias map + extraction-time enum validation with drop accounting
ideas.detail: Mechanism tags get TAG_ALIASES but stylized facts have no alias map and no validation anywhere between the LLM and the matrices; near-miss labels are silently discarded at three independent call sites (coverage, gap view A, dashboard). Cheapest path: a FACT_ALIASES dict in taxonomy.py applied inside extract_paper_structured (unknowns → 'other' + a logged warning), plus a 'dropped_fact_tokens' counter in build_coverage's return dict surfaced in coverage.md.

ideas.title: A `doctor` / check-db subcommand for DB invariants
ideas.detail: Most crashes found trace to unvalidated DB states: missing runs table (gap-mine crash), vN-suffixed arxiv_ids, oa: rows without source_kind='openalex', empty idea_text (bricks idea list), out-of-enum facts. A read-only `arxiv_cli --db X doctor` that checks each invariant and prints the fixing command (strip-arxiv-versions etc.) would turn tracebacks into actionable reports.

ideas.title: Golden-number regression test for coverage artifacts
ideas.detail: The totals-row misalignment and the PNG-vs-markdown '(n)' disagreement would be caught immediately by one test that builds a ~10-row fixture with hand-computed expected matrix/row/col totals and asserts against both render_markdown output (parsed cells, header/row cell-count equality) and build_coverage's dict. None of the 30 test files pins the markdown table shape today.

ideas.title: Per-subfield self-match test for the gap tensor
ideas.detail: For each of the 25 SUBFIELDS, synthesize a paper tagged with the subfield's own canonical slug (hyphenated) and assert _matches_subfield / view A row_total > 0. This directly catches the space-vs-hyphen needle bug for all subfields at once and guards future subfields.py edits. Same harness should assert view C joins on a technique ref_paper present in the fixture.

ideas.title: Offline CLI smoke matrix in CI
ideas.detail: Every read-only subcommand (list/show/search/coverage/gap-mine/dashboard/stylized-fact-other/extract-untagged --dry-run/delete-low-relevance/strip-arxiv-versions --dry-run, propose_cli list/show, idea_cli list) run against two fixture DBs — literature-only and literature+runs — asserting exit codes and no tracebacks. This session's harness found four crashes in under a minute with zero network; wiring it into one workflow would prevent regressions the unit tests miss because they never go through argparse/main().

ideas.title: Golden hand-computed corpus regression test for the full atlas numerics
ideas.detail: Existing tests assert isolated cells; nothing pins the whole pipeline (row selection, family banding, other-suppression, diagonal exclusion, totals, markdown alignment) against a known answer. My 12-paper synthetic corpus reproduced the entire expected matrix by hand and immediately exposed the markdown misalignment — committing it as tests/test_coverage_golden.py (assert full matrix, all totals dicts, and per-row markdown cell counts) would freeze the contract cheaply.

ideas.title: Vocabulary-drift telemetry at ingest and build time
ideas.detail: Off-enum stylized facts and unmatched mechanism tags are silently discarded in three places (coverage intersect, view A membership test, kept-row filter). Add counters: at extraction, tally facts rejected by CANONICAL_FACTS; at build_coverage, report n_facts_dropped and n_papers_unclassified in the returned dict and dashboard header. Without this, an LLM model swap can hollow out the atlas invisibly.

ideas.title: Subfield-matcher self-validation harness
ideas.detail: validate_subfields() checks key uniqueness and seed id shapes but not matchability. Add a check that every subfield's title_any matches at least one canonical taxonomy slug (and, opportunistically, at least one live corpus paper), and that no needle is a substring of an unrelated canonical tag ('market' vs 'market-making'). This would have caught both the hyphenisation dead-rows and the generic-token pollution before they shaped gap proposals.

ideas.title: Robust, honest scale estimation for view B
ideas.detail: View B currently uses pooled cross-asset population std over as few as 1-12 real points, with 1.0 fallback and silent dim-dropping. Replace with a per-dim robust scale (MAD or IQR over all real windows), mark cells NaN when unmeasurable (render as N/A like the coverage diagonal), and attach a bootstrap CI over the ~12 family runs so a 1.8-sigma and a 12.2-sigma cell are distinguishable from noise before they drive proposal generation.

ideas.title: Expected-count baseline for gap salience instead of log(row+col+1)
ideas.detail: Salience currently rewards any zero cell in proportion to added row+col mass, so the 'other'-column mass and multi-label duplicates inflate row density, and a col_total=1 cell can outrank a col_total=6 cell purely on row size. A chi-square/PMI-style score, log(expected+1)-log(observed+1) with expected = row_tot*col_tot/total, would rank 'surprisingly empty' cells rather than 'cells adjacent to bulk', and make the A/C vs B weighting comparable. Include a top-N stability report (does the ranking survive removing one paper?).

ideas.title: Make the CI lint gate real (remove `|| true`, pin an error-class ruleset)
ideas.detail: ci.yml runs ruff and discards the exit code, which is why an F821 NameError reachable from a CI workflow (atlas_refresh canon-atlas step) shipped. Cheapest path: split the step into a hard gate `ruff check packages tests --select F,E9,PLE` (currently 100% clean except the one real bug) and an advisory run for experiments/src. Fixing the one F821 plus deleting `|| true` is under an hour and permanently prevents this class.

ideas.title: CLI cold-path smoke tests: run every subcommand's parser + handler wiring
ideas.detail: arxiv_cli has 33 subcommands dispatched through a hand-maintained dict; methods_cli has 11 if-branches. Nothing exercises error paths (the canon-atlas NameError lives in one). Add a parametrized test that (a) asserts parser-name/handler-dict parity by introspection, and (b) invokes each subcommand with --help, plus targeted tests of failure branches with mocked network layers. Would have caught the reported bug and catches future dispatch drift.

ideas.title: Packaging isolation check: install the fingerprint-atlas wheel with only its declared deps
ideas.detail: The undeclared-matplotlib breakage is invisible because CI always `uv sync`s the whole workspace. A CI job that builds the wheel, installs it into a bare venv (with local abm-models/stylized-facts wheels), imports every module, and calls one plotting entry point would catch any declared-vs-imported dependency drift automatically. `uv build && uv run --isolated` or a 20-line script over importlib suffices.

ideas.title: Fingerprint vector schema versioning in the runs table
ideas.detail: FEATURE_NAMES already grew 6→9 (v3→v4) with no stored schema marker; load_runs deserializes blindly and consumers hard-unpack 9 values. Store `feature_names` (or a schema_version int) in provenance_json or a column at insert_run time, and have load_runs validate/migrate. Without this, the next feature addition silently corrupts distance matrices computed over mixed-era rows or crashes label/propose CLIs on archived DBs.

ideas.title: Run the shipped catalog validators as pytest gates
ideas.detail: validate_subfields() and validate_techniques() encode real integrity invariants (unique subfield keys, well-formed arxiv/DOI/OpenAlex refs across the 25-subfield and 30-technique catalogs) but have zero callers. Two 3-line tests asserting empty-warnings turn them from dead code into regression gates for the hand-curated data that drives canon-atlas file naming and dashboard reference links.

ideas.title: Row-normalized coverage heatmap alongside absolute counts (reviewer demand #1 — still absent)
ideas.detail: Verified absent: coverage.py renders only absolute counts (imshow vmin=0.5..vmax) and no row-share variant exists anywhere in the package. Absolute cells conflate corpus composition with per-mechanism fact emphasis: 'minority-game x leverage = 0' is uninterpretable without knowing whether MG has 3 or 60 papers. Why it matters for defense: every 'mechanism X never targets fact Y' claim is only defensible as a conditional share. Cheapest path: in render_heatmap add a second panel (or `normalize='row'` kwarg) plotting M / np.array(cov['row_papers'])[:,None] with % annotations, reusing the existing excluded-cells/NaN machinery; emit both PNGs from cmd_coverage.

ideas.title: LLM-extraction spot-check protocol with human gold labels + agreement stats (reviewer demand #3 — fully absent)
ideas.detail: Verified absent: label_cli.py is run-preference labeling (unrelated); no gold-label table, no agreement metric, no sampling command exists for literature extraction. All coverage/gap/proposal claims rest on unvalidated LLM tags — the first committee question. Cheapest path: `arxiv_cli spot-check --n 30` samples rows stratified by method_family and extraction era, writes a review markdown (title/abstract + blank mechanism_tags/facts fields), imports human answers into a new literature_gold table, and reports per-fact precision/recall plus Cohen's kappa (per-label, multi-label micro-averaged) against the stored LLM extraction. Persist the agreement report with a date so the thesis can cite 'kappa=0.xx on a 30-paper stratified audit'.

ideas.title: 'other'-bucket decomposition into candidate new fact columns (reviewer demand #4 — half done)
ideas.detail: The stylized-fact-other CLI (arxiv_cli.py:670) audits/groups/retags 'other' papers by mechanism tag and finds delete candidates, but nothing mines WHAT those papers actually target to propose new canonical columns. Why it matters: the taxonomy comment itself routes real phenomena (e.g. multifractality of returns) into 'other'; a defense claim 'these 9 facts span the corpus' needs evidence the residual bucket is unstructured. Cheapest path: extend cmd_stylized_fact_other with a --mine mode running TF-IDF/bigram counts over mechanism_summary+title+novelty_signal of 'other'-tagged rows, emitting ranked candidate fact terms with paper counts; human promotes winners into CANONICAL_FACTS and reruns --retag.

ideas.title: Out-of-enum fact-string monitor (silent-drop counter in build_coverage / view A)
ideas.detail: extract_paper_structured never validates stylized_facts_targeted against CANONICAL_FACTS (arxiv_ingest.py:441 just splits/truncates), and unknown strings vanish silently at coverage.py:127 (`facts &= set(cols)`) and gap_finder view A (`if f in facts`). A prompt regression emitting 'heavy-tails' instead of 'fat-tails' would zero a column with no warning, fabricating a gap. Cheapest path: have build_coverage also return a Counter of dropped raw strings; print it in cmd_coverage and fail CI when the drop rate exceeds a threshold; log unknown strings at extraction time.

ideas.title: Missing-not-at-random caveat + classification funnel on rendered artifacts (reviewer demand #5 — half done)
ideas.detail: The misleading all-ingested denominator was removed from the heatmap title and coverage.md shows 'X / Y papers classified', but no rendered artifact states that unclassified papers are systematically missing (extraction skips relevance<0.3 rows, gives up after max attempts, and OA-only rows lack PDFs) — so empty cells may reflect extraction failure modes, not literature gaps. Cheapest path: a one-line footnote on the heatmap and coverage.md ('unclassified papers are not missing at random; see extraction_attempts / source_kind'), plus a small funnel table per source_kind: ingested -> extracted -> fact-mapped -> in-matrix, computed from columns already in literature_methods.

ideas.title: Provenance stamps (DB content hash + git SHA + timestamp + row counts) on every figure and page
ideas.detail: Nothing generated carries provenance: the dashboard footer is a static 'Static snapshot · local workspace', PNGs have no build metadata, and the dashboard silently copies whatever canon_atlas.html it finds — including the documented all-ERROR one from the 2026-06-29 OpenAlex outage — with no staleness marker. The repo README itself (line 57) mandates run_id/timestamp/git_commit on results. Cheapest path: a tiny provenance.stamp(db_path) helper returning {sqlite md5, git rev-parse --short HEAD, UTC time, n_papers, n_runs}; append it to fig titles/footers in render_heatmap, canon_atlas subtitle, dashboard _page footer, and coverage.md header, and write manifest.json next to each dashboard build. Any thesis figure then traces to an exact corpus state.

ideas.title: Gap-ranking stability under bootstrap and vocabulary perturbation
ideas.detail: rank_top_gaps emits a point ranking with zero uncertainty; a thesis claim 'Prospect-theory x regime-switching is an open gap' should survive resampling. With a few-hundred-paper corpus, single papers flip cells between 0 and 1 and reorder salience. Cheapest path: `gap-mine --bootstrap 200` resamples rows with replacement, recomputes build_views + rank_top_gaps, and reports each gap's inclusion frequency in the top-20 plus salience CI; secondarily jackknife TAG_ALIASES/subfield title_any entries to show gaps aren't artifacts of one vocabulary choice. Report only gaps with inclusion >= 0.8 in the dashboard table.

ideas.title: Per-cell statistical null (Fisher/permutation) for 'empty cell in dense row' gaps
ideas.detail: The salience score log(row+col+1)-log(cell+1) has no calibration: given the margins, many zero cells are expected by chance, and a committee will ask exactly that. Cheapest path: for each view-A/C zero cell compute the hypergeometric probability of observing 0 given row/column margins and grand total (closed form, no simulation), or permute fact-label assignments 1000x; annotate each ranked gap with its empirical p-value and drop cells where an empty value is unremarkable. This converts 'the cell is empty' into 'the cell is emptier than chance at p<0.05' — a defensible statement.

ideas.title: Extraction-era A/B measurement and multi-label verification at scale (reviewer demand #2 follow-through)
ideas.detail: Multi-label extraction shipped in the prompt (arxiv_ingest.py:223) and mean_facts_per_paper is displayed, but the only version-controlled corpus (unmerged CI branch) has 9 extracted rows with mean 1.0 facts/paper — the contract is unverified at scale, and extracted_by_model is stored but never analyzed. Different eras (gpt-oss-120b vs gpt-4o-mini, pre/post multi-label prompt) are mixed in one matrix, so column growth may be an extraction artifact, not literature signal. Cheapest path: add a prompt_version constant persisted per row; `arxiv_cli extraction-report` grouping mean facts/paper, tag entropy, 'other' share by (extracted_by_model, prompt_version); re-extract a fixed 20-paper panel under each era and report pairwise label agreement.

ideas.title: Citation-weighted coverage variant
ideas.detail: Cells count papers equally: a cell 'covered' by one 3-citation preprint reads identically to one anchored by Cont 2001. For gap claims ('underexplored relative to attention'), influence weighting is the natural robustness check. Cheapest path: build_coverage already receives rows containing oa_cited_by_count; add weight=log1p(cites) accumulation into a parallel matrix and render it as a second panel; a gap that persists under both unweighted and citation-weighted views is a much stronger defense exhibit.

ideas.title: Prior-art defense dossier for the user's OWN novelty claims (external sweep in idea_judge)
ideas.detail: idea_judge ranks only the local DB (methods/literature_methods/proposals, pure keyword overlap) before issuing 'genuinely_novel' verdicts — a verdict only as good as local ingestion coverage, which canon-atlas shows is partial. A defense needs evidence that an external search was performed and dated. Cheapest path: after aspect extraction, run key_keywords through openalex search (find_canon_papers already exists) and semantic_scholar, store top-N titles/ids/years + query + timestamp as prior_art_json on the ideas row, and inject them into the verdict call; render the dossier in idea_cli show. Turns 'the LLM thinks it's novel' into 'novel against local corpus AND a dated OpenAlex/S2 sweep'.

ideas.title: Canon-vs-corpus recall measurement (how much of the corpus the 25 subfields can even see)
ideas.detail: Canon-atlas measures precision-style coverage (canon in DB per subfield) but nothing measures the converse: what fraction of literature_methods rows match NO subfield, i.e. the corpus mass invisible to gap view A (whose rows are subfields). If 40% of papers are unmatched, view-A gaps are computed on a biased slice. Cheapest path: reuse gap_finder._matches_subfield over all rows x SUBFIELDS, report unmatched % and top tags among unmatched papers in cmd_gap_mine output and the dashboard; unmatched clusters are also the strongest candidates for new subfield entries.

ideas.title: Canon search snapshot cache + --offline mode (implement docs/canon_atlas_next_actions.md)
ideas.detail: Already specified in-repo with schema and acceptance tests but unimplemented: every canon-atlas run re-queries OpenAlex, so coverage numbers vary with API outages and ranking drift (the 2026-06-29 all-ERROR atlas is documented), and results are irreproducible. Cheapest path: implement the two tables exactly as specified (canon_search_snapshots / canon_search_results incl. rejected candidates), default to newest successful snapshot, add --refresh/--offline, and re-render HTML after --auto-ingest-missing. This also makes canon coverage diffable over time.

ideas.title: Coverage/gap history time-series table
ideas.detail: Every build overwrites the previous picture; there is no record of how coverage or the top-gap set evolved across ingests, so 'we closed gap X by ingesting preset Y' cannot be evidenced, and regressions (e.g. a retag wiping a column) are invisible. Cheapest path: on each cmd_coverage/gap-mine run, append one row to a coverage_history table (timestamp, db hash, n_papers, n_classified, per-fact col totals JSON, top-20 gap keys JSON); add a small dashboard sparkline panel. Pairs with the provenance stamps to give the thesis a documented corpus-growth narrative.

ideas.title: Preprint/journal duplicate collapse before coverage denominators
ideas.detail: Promised in docs/canon_atlas_next_actions.md ('Deduplicate journal/preprint variants by DOI, normalized title, and OpenAlex merged-work relationships') and absent: a paper ingested via arxiv AND via canon_ingest (oa:W-id) counts twice in literature_methods (different arxiv_id keys), double-counting matrix cells and canon denominators. Cheapest path: a dedup pass keyed on (DOI, casefolded-title) at load_literature time that merges rows preferring the arxiv-sourced one, plus a CLI report of suspected duplicates for manual confirmation.

ideas.title: Repo merge-policy guardrails: disable squash merges, protect main
ideas.detail: The whole cron-PR ecosystem (ingest/propose/annotate/atlas branches based on merged history) only works if feature merges preserve ancestry — empirically, squashing PR #7 turns two clean cron PRs into 6 and 20 conflicts. Nothing in the repo records this constraint. Cheapest path: repo Settings -> disable 'Squash' and 'Rebase' merge buttons, add branch protection on main requiring the 'test' check, and a CONTRIBUTING.md line explaining why merge commits are mandatory here.

ideas.title: Cron-PR janitor: dedupe snapshots and auto-close superseded data PRs
ideas.detail: Two weekly ingest runs produced byte-different but semantically identical snapshots (same 30 arxiv ids) and one is stranded without a PR. Add a step before create-pull-request that diffs the new snapshot's arxiv_id set against the checked-out one and skips PR creation when the delta is empty; add a small scheduled job that lists ingest-arxiv/* and propose/* branches with no open PR older than 14 days and deletes or reports them.

ideas.title: Close the CI state loop: import merged annotations/snapshots before queue selection
ideas.detail: Both stateful crons rebuild their DB from seed each run and never read back their own merged outputs, so methods_annotate will re-draft the same method daily and ingest re-pays extraction until a snapshot PR lands. Add `methods_cli import-md methods_drafts/**/*.md` to the annotate workflow's rebuild step, and make the ingest workflow fail loudly (or auto-merge to a data branch) when data/literature_methods.json is absent after the first successful run, so the accumulation contract is enforced rather than assumed.

ideas.title: Post-merge cutover checklist encoded as a tracked doc + stale-branch report
ideas.detail: This merge has a time-critical, multi-step cutover (merge PR #7 before Sunday's cron, delete head branch via UI so PRs #3/#4 retarget, merge/close 5 other branches, fix .gitignore) that currently lives nowhere. Add docs/RELEASING.md with the ordered checklist, and a monthly scheduled workflow that posts an issue listing every remote branch with ahead/behind counts and last-commit age — this repo accumulated 9 remote branches in 3 weeks with three different 'spec 002's, so drift is fast.

ideas.title: Spec-number registry to prevent parallel-agent collisions
